title: hieracium macilentum subsp. exilentum
intro: Hieracium macilentum subsp. exilentum, also known as Mountain Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The plant is rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which are thought to provide health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to protect against cell damage and infections. Hieracium macilentum subsp. exilentum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with some herbal practitioners recommending its use for topical application to soothe skin irritations and promote healing.
